LAW AND MOTION 301, ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS -- DEFENDANT FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY'S NTC OF MOTION TO DISMISS OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE STAY THE ACTION IS DENIED WITH THE PARTIES STIPULATION THAT PLAINTIFF WILL PROVIDE IT'S WITNESSES IN CALIFORNIA AT PLAINTIFF'S EXPENSE. Off Calendar Sep-08-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Melissa Quigley, Esq. og Wilson & McQueeny, 818-547-6504 for defendant and moving party Fireman's Fund Insurance Company
Jacob N. Foster, Esq. and Charles N. Frieberg, Esq., 415-421-6140 for plaintiff and opposing party EFCO Corporation

RULING:
ARGUED and the Court MODIFIED its tentative ruling as follows --
DEFENDANT FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY'S Ntc Of Motion To Dismiss Or In The Alternative Stay The Action IS DENIED WITH THE PARTIES STIPULATION THAT PLAINTIFF WILL PROVIDE IT'S WITNESSES IN CALIFORNIA AT PLAINTIFF'S EXPENSE. THE PRIVATE AND PUBLIC FACTORS DO NOT SUFFICIENTLY WEIGH AGAINST PLAINTIFF'S CHOICE OF FORUM FOR A SUIT AGAINST THIS CALIFORNIA RESIDENT. Plaintiff's counsel to submit form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 (301/PJB)
